"""
This file contains the robosuite environment wrapper that is used
to provide a standardized environment API for training policies and interacting
with metadata present in datasets.
"""
import json
import numpy as np
from copy import deepcopy

import robosuite
import robocasa.utils.robomimic.robomimic_obs_utils as ObsUtils


class EnvRobocasa:
    """Wrapper class for robosuite environments (https://github.com/ARISE-Initiative/robosuite)"""

    def __init__(
        self,
        env_name,
        render=False,
        render_offscreen=False,
        use_image_obs=False,
        postprocess_visual_obs=True,
        env_lang=None,
        **kwargs,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            env_name (str): name of environment. Only needs to be provided if making a different
                environment from the one in @env_meta.

            render (bool): if True, environment supports on-screen rendering

            render_offscreen (bool): if True, environment supports off-screen rendering. This
                is forced to be True if @env_meta["use_images"] is True.

            use_image_obs (bool): if True, environment is expected to render rgb image observations
                on every env.step call. Set this to False for efficiency reasons, if image
                observations are not required.

            postprocess_visual_obs (bool): if True, postprocess image observations
                to prepare for learning. This should only be False when extracting observations
                for saving to a dataset (to save space on RGB images for example).

            lang: TODO add documentation
        """
        self.postprocess_visual_obs = postprocess_visual_obs

        # robosuite version check
        self._is_v1 = robosuite.__version__.split(".")[0] == "1"
        if self._is_v1:
            assert (
                int(robosuite.__version__.split(".")[1]) >= 2
            ), "only support robosuite v0.3 and v1.2+"

        kwargs = deepcopy(kwargs)

        # update kwargs based on passed arguments
        update_kwargs = dict(
            has_renderer=render,
            has_offscreen_renderer=(render_offscreen or use_image_obs),
            ignore_done=True,
            use_object_obs=True,
            use_camera_obs=use_image_obs,
            camera_depths=False,
        )
        kwargs.update(update_kwargs)

        if self._is_v1:
            if kwargs["has_offscreen_renderer"]:
                """
                remove reliance on egl_probe, may cause issues
                # ensure that we select the correct GPU device for rendering by testing for EGL rendering
                # NOTE: this package should be installed from this link (https://github.com/StanfordVL/egl_probe)
                import egl_probe
                valid_gpu_devices = egl_probe.get_available_devices()
                if len(valid_gpu_devices) > 0:
                    kwargs["render_gpu_device_id"] = valid_gpu_devices[0]
                """
                pass
        else:
            # make sure gripper visualization is turned off (we almost always want this for learning)
            kwargs["gripper_visualization"] = False
            del kwargs["camera_depths"]
            kwargs["camera_depth"] = False  # rename kwarg

        self._env_name = env_name
        self._init_kwargs = deepcopy(kwargs)
        self.env = robosuite.make(self._env_name, **kwargs)
        self.base_env = self.env  # for mimicgen
        self.env_lang = env_lang

        if self._is_v1:
            # Make sure joint position observations and eef vel observations are active
            for ob_name in self.env.observation_names:
                if ("joint_pos" in ob_name) or ("eef_vel" in ob_name):
                    self.env.modify_observable(
                        observable_name=ob_name, attribute="active", modifier=True
                    )

    def step(self, action):
        """
        Step in the environment with an action.

        Args:
            action (np.array): action to take

        Returns:
            observation (dict): new observation dictionary
            reward (float): reward for this step
            done (bool): whether the task is done
            info (dict): extra information
        """
        obs, r, done, info = self.env.step(action)
        obs = self.get_observation(obs)
        info["is_success"] = self.is_success()
        return obs, r, self.is_done(), info

    def reset(self, unset_ep_meta=True):
        """
        Reset environment.

        Args:
            unset_ep_meta (np.array): whether to unset any episode meta data

        Returns:
            observation (dict): initial observation dictionary.
        """
        if unset_ep_meta and hasattr(self.env, "unset_ep_meta"):
            # unset the ep meta to clear out any ep meta that was previously set
            self.env.unset_ep_meta()

        di = self.env.reset()

        # keep track of episode language and embedding
        if self.env_lang is not None:
            self._ep_lang_str = self.env_lang
        elif hasattr(self.env, "get_ep_meta"):
            # get ep_meta if applicable
            ep_meta = self.env.get_ep_meta()
            self._ep_lang_str = ep_meta.get("lang", "dummy")
        else:
            self._ep_lang_str = "dummy"

        # self._ep_lang_emb = LangUtils.get_lang_emb(self._ep_lang_str)

        return self.get_observation(di)

    # notifies the environment whether or not the next environemnt testing object should update its category
    def update_env(self, attr, value):
        setattr(self.env, attr, value)

    def reset_to(self, state):
        """
        Reset to a specific simulator state.

        Args:
            state (dict): current simulator state that contains one or more of:
                - states (np.ndarray): initial state of the mujoco environment
                - model (str): mujoco scene xml

        Returns:
            observation (dict): observation dictionary after setting the simulator state (only
                if "states" is in @state)
        """
        should_ret = False
        if "model" in state:
            if state.get("ep_meta", None) is not None:
                # set relevant episode information
                ep_meta = json.loads(state["ep_meta"])
            else:
                ep_meta = {}

            if hasattr(
                self.env, "set_attrs_from_ep_meta"
            ):  # older versions had this function
                self.env.set_attrs_from_ep_meta(ep_meta)
            elif hasattr(self.env, "set_ep_meta"):  # newer versions
                self.env.set_ep_meta(ep_meta)
            # this reset is necessary.
            # while the call to env.reset_from_xml_string does call reset,
            # that is only a "soft" reset that doesn't actually reload the model.
            self.reset(unset_ep_meta=False)
            robosuite_version_id = int(robosuite.__version__.split(".")[1])
            if robosuite_version_id <= 3:
                from robosuite.utils.mjcf_utils import postprocess_model_xml

                xml = postprocess_model_xml(state["model"])
            else:
                # v1.4 and above use the class-based edit_model_xml function
                xml = self.env.edit_model_xml(state["model"])

            self.env.reset_from_xml_string(xml)
            self.env.sim.reset()
            if not self._is_v1:
                # hide teleop visualization after restoring from model
                self.env.sim.model.site_rgba[self.env.eef_site_id] = np.array(
                    [0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]
                )
                self.env.sim.model.site_rgba[self.env.eef_cylinder_id] = np.array(
                    [0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]
                )
        if "states" in state:
            self.env.sim.set_state_from_flattened(state["states"])
            self.env.sim.forward()
            should_ret = True

        # update state as needed
        if hasattr(self.env, "update_sites"):
            # older versions of environment had update_sites function
            self.env.update_sites()
        if hasattr(self.env, "update_state"):
            # later versions renamed this to update_state
            self.env.update_state()

        if "goal" in state:
            self.set_goal(**state["goal"])
        if should_ret:
            # only return obs if we've done a forward call - otherwise the observations will be garbage
            return self.get_observation()
        return None

    def render(self, mode="human", height=None, width=None, camera_name=None):
        """
        Render from simulation to either an on-screen window or off-screen to RGB array.

        Args:
            mode (str): pass "human" for on-screen rendering or "rgb_array" for off-screen rendering
            height (int): height of image to render - only used if mode is "rgb_array"
            width (int): width of image to render - only used if mode is "rgb_array"
            camera_name (str): camera name to use for rendering
        """
        # if camera_name is None, infer from initial env kwargs
        if camera_name is None:
            camera_name = self._init_kwargs.get("camera_names", ["agentview"])[0]

        if mode == "human":
            cam_id = self.env.sim.model.camera_name2id(camera_name)
            self.env.viewer.set_camera(cam_id)
            return self.env.render()
        elif mode == "rgb_array":
            return self.env.sim.render(
                height=height, width=width, camera_name=camera_name
            )[::-1]
        else:
            raise NotImplementedError("mode={} is not implemented".format(mode))

    def get_observation(self, di=None):
        """
        Get current environment observation dictionary.

        Args:
            di (dict): current raw observation dictionary from robosuite to wrap and provide
                as a dictionary. If not provided, will be queried from robosuite.
        """
        if di is None:
            di = (
                self.env._get_observations(force_update=True)
                if self._is_v1
                else self.env._get_observation()
            )
        ret = {}
        for k in di:
            if (k in ObsUtils.OBS_KEYS_TO_MODALITIES) and ObsUtils.key_is_obs_modality(
                key=k, obs_modality="rgb"
            ):
                ret[k] = di[k][::-1]
                if self.postprocess_visual_obs:
                    ret[k] = ObsUtils.process_obs(obs=ret[k], obs_key=k)

        # "object" key contains object information
        if "object-state" in di:
            ret["object"] = np.array(di["object-state"])

        if self._is_v1:
            for robot in self.env.robots:
                # add all robot-arm-specific observations. Note the (k not in ret) check
                # ensures that we don't accidentally add robot wrist images a second time
                pf = robot.robot_model.naming_prefix
                for k in di:
                    if (
                        k.startswith(pf)
                        and (k not in ret)
                        and (not k.endswith("proprio-state"))
                    ):
                        ret[k] = np.array(di[k])
        else:
            # minimal proprioception for older versions of robosuite
            ret["proprio"] = np.array(di["robot-state"])
            ret["eef_pos"] = np.array(di["eef_pos"])
            ret["eef_quat"] = np.array(di["eef_quat"])
            ret["gripper_qpos"] = np.array(di["gripper_qpos"])

        # ret["lang_emb"] = np.array(self._ep_lang_emb)
        return ret

    def get_state(self):
        """
        Get current environment simulator state as a dictionary. Should be compatible with @reset_to.
        """
        xml = self.env.sim.model.get_xml()  # model xml file
        state = np.array(self.env.sim.get_state().flatten())  # simulator state
        info = dict(model=xml, states=state)
        if hasattr(self.env, "get_ep_meta"):
            # get ep_meta if applicable
            info["ep_meta"] = json.dumps(self.env.get_ep_meta(), indent=4)
        return info

    def get_reward(self):
        """
        Get current reward.
        """
        return self.env.reward()

    def get_goal(self):
        """
        Get goal observation. Not all environments support this.
        """
        return self.get_observation(self.env._get_goal())

    def set_goal(self, **kwargs):
        """
        Set goal observation with external specification. Not all environments support this.
        """
        return self.env.set_goal(**kwargs)

    def is_done(self):
        """
        Check if the task is done (not necessarily successful).
        """

        # Robosuite envs always rollout to fixed horizon.
        return False

    def is_success(self):
        """
        Check if the task condition(s) is reached. Should return a dictionary
        { str: bool } with at least a "task" key for the overall task success,
        and additional optional keys corresponding to other task criteria.
        """
        succ = self.env._check_success()
        if isinstance(succ, dict):
            assert "task" in succ
            return succ
        return {"task": succ}

    @property
    def action_dimension(self):
        """
        Returns dimension of actions (int).
        """
        return self.env.action_spec[0].shape[0]

    @property
    def name(self):
        """
        Returns name of environment name (str).
        """
        return self._env_name

    @property
    def type(self):
        """
        Returns environment type (int) for this kind of environment.
        This helps identify this env class.
        """
        # return EB.EnvType.ROBOSUITE_TYPE
        return 1  # hardcode value for now

    @property
    def version(self):
        """
        Returns version of robosuite used for this environment, eg. 1.2.0
        """
        return robosuite.__version__

    def serialize(self):
        """
        Save all information needed to re-instantiate this environment in a dictionary.
        This is the same as @env_meta - environment metadata stored in hdf5 datasets,
        and used in utils/env_utils.py.
        """
        return dict(
            env_name=self.name,
            env_version=self.version,
            type=self.type,
            env_kwargs=deepcopy(self._init_kwargs),
        )

    @classmethod
    def create_for_data_processing(
        cls,
        env_name,
        camera_names,
        camera_height,
        camera_width,
        reward_shaping,
        **kwargs,
    ):
        """
        Create environment for processing datasets, which includes extracting
        observations, labeling dense / sparse rewards, and annotating dones in
        transitions.

        Args:
            env_name (str): name of environment
            camera_names (list of str): list of camera names that correspond to image observations
            camera_height (int): camera height for all cameras
            camera_width (int): camera width for all cameras
            reward_shaping (bool): if True, use shaped environment rewards, else use sparse task completion rewards
        """
        is_v1 = robosuite.__version__.split(".")[0] == "1"
        has_camera = len(camera_names) > 0

        new_kwargs = {
            "reward_shaping": reward_shaping,
        }

        if has_camera:
            if is_v1:
                new_kwargs["camera_names"] = list(camera_names)
                new_kwargs["camera_heights"] = camera_height
                new_kwargs["camera_widths"] = camera_width
            else:
                assert len(camera_names) == 1
                if has_camera:
                    new_kwargs["camera_name"] = camera_names[0]
                    new_kwargs["camera_height"] = camera_height
                    new_kwargs["camera_width"] = camera_width

        kwargs.update(new_kwargs)

        # also initialize obs utils so it knows which modalities are image modalities
        image_modalities = list(camera_names)
        if is_v1:
            image_modalities = ["{}_image".format(cn) for cn in camera_names]
        elif has_camera:
            # v0.3 only had support for one image, and it was named "rgb"
            assert len(image_modalities) == 1
            image_modalities = ["rgb"]
        obs_modality_specs = {
            "obs": {
                "low_dim": [],  # technically unused, so we don't have to specify all of them
                "rgb": image_modalities,
            }
        }
        ObsUtils.initialize_obs_utils_with_obs_specs(obs_modality_specs)

        # note that @postprocess_visual_obs is False since this env's images will be written to a dataset
        return cls(
            env_name=env_name,
            render=False,
            render_offscreen=has_camera,
            use_image_obs=has_camera,
            postprocess_visual_obs=False,
            **kwargs,
        )

    @property
    def rollout_exceptions(self):
        """
        Return tuple of exceptions to except when doing rollouts. This is useful to ensure
        that the entire training run doesn't crash because of a bad policy that causes unstable
        simulation computations.
        """
        return Exception

    def __repr__(self):
        """
        Pretty-print env description.
        """
        return (
            self.name + "\n" + json.dumps(self._init_kwargs, sort_keys=True, indent=4)
        )

{"path": "/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/robocasa_camera_movement.py", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Robocasa Camera Movement Example Script

This script demonstrates how to dynamically set camera poses at each timestep
in Robocasa for collecting training data for next best view selection models.

The camera poses are expressed in absolute coordinates in the robot base frame.

Usage:
    python robocasa_camera_movement.py

Output:
    - camera_trajectory.mp4: Video showing smooth camera trajectory movement
"""

import os
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R
import cv2

# Suppress robosuite warnings
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning)

import robosuite
from robosuite.utils.camera_utils import CameraMover
from robocasa.environments.kitchen.composite.pick_place_counter_to_cabinet import PickPlaceCounterToCabinet


def create_camera_trajectory(num_frames=300, radius=1.5, height_range=(0.8, 1.5)):
    """
    Create a smooth camera trajectory around the robot workspace.
    
    Args:
        num_frames: Number of frames in the trajectory
        radius: Radius of the circular trajectory around the robot
        height_range: (min_height, max_height) for vertical movement
        
    Returns:
        List of (position, quaternion) tuples for each frame
    """
    trajectory = []
    
    # Robot base position (approximately where the robot is located)
    robot_base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    for i in range(num_frames):
        # Calculate progress through trajectory (0 to 1)
        t = i / num_frames
        
        # Circular motion around the robot
        angle = 2 * np.pi * t
        
        # Position: circular path with varying height
        x = robot_base_pos[0] + radius * np.cos(angle)
        y = robot_base_pos[1] + radius * np.sin(angle)
        z = height_range[0] + (height_range[1] - height_range[0]) * 0.5 * (1 + np.sin(2 * np.pi * t))
        
        pos = np.array([x, y, z])
        
        # Orientation: always look at the robot base
        # Calculate direction vector from camera to target
        direction = robot_base_pos - pos
        direction[2] += 0.3  # Look slightly above the base
        
        # Create rotation matrix that looks at the target
        # Up vector is approximately +z
        up = np.array([0, 0, 1])
        
        # Calculate right, up, forward vectors
        forward = direction / np.linalg.norm(direction)
        right = np.cross(forward, up)
        right = right / (np.linalg.norm(right) + 1e-8)
        up = np.cross(right, forward)
        
        # Create rotation matrix
        rot_matrix = np.column_stack([right, up, forward])
        
        # Convert to quaternion (xyzw format for robosuite)
        quat_xyzw = R.from_matrix(rot_matrix).as_quat()
        
        trajectory.append((pos, quat_xyzw))
    
    return trajectory


def create_spiral_trajectory(num_frames=300, start_radius=2.0, end_radius=0.8, 
                              start_height=1.5, end_height=0.6, num_rotations=2):
    """
    Create a spiral camera trajectory that moves inward toward the robot.
    
    Args:
        num_frames: Number of frames in the trajectory
        start_radius: Starting radius of the spiral
        end_radius: Ending radius of the spiral
        start_height: Starting height
        end_height: Ending height
        num_rotations: Number of complete rotations in the spiral
        
    Returns:
        List of (position, quaternion) tuples for each frame
    """
    trajectory = []
    
    robot_base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    for i in range(num_frames):
        t = i / num_frames
        
        # Spiral parameters
        angle = 2 * np.pi * num_rotations * t
        radius = start_radius + (end_radius - start_radius) * t
        height = start_height + (end_height - start_height) * t
        
        # Position
        x = robot_base_pos[0] + radius * np.cos(angle)
        y = robot_base_pos[1] + radius * np.sin(angle)
        z = height
        
        pos = np.array([x, y, z])
        
        # Orientation: look at robot base
        direction = robot_base_pos - pos
        direction[2] += 0.2
        
        up = np.array([0, 0, 1])
        forward = direction / np.linalg.norm(direction)
        right = np.cross(forward, up)
        right = right / (np.linalg.norm(right) + 1e-8)
        up = np.cross(right, forward)
        
        rot_matrix = np.column_stack([right, up, forward])
        quat_xyzw = R.from_matrix(rot_matrix).as_quat()
        
        trajectory.append((pos, quat_xyzw))
    
    return trajectory


def set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(env, camera_mover, pos_robot_base, quat_xyzw_robot_base):
    """
    Set camera pose using coordinates in the robot base frame.
    
    This function transforms the camera pose from the robot base frame to the
    world frame before setting it.
    
    Args:
        env: Robocasa environment
        camera_mover: CameraMover instance
        pos_robot_base: Camera position in robot base frame (x, y, z)
        quat_xyzw_robot_base: Camera orientation in robot base frame (x, y, z, w)
    """
    # Get robot base position in world frame
    # In Robocasa, the robot base is typically at a known offset from world origin
    # For most kitchen environments, we need to get the mobile base position
    
    # Get the robot's base position
    robot = env.env.robots[0]
    
    # Get robot base position in world frame
    # The mobile base position can be obtained from the robot model
    try:
        # Try to get mobile base body
        base_body_name = robot.robot_model.naming_prefix + "mobilebase"
        base_pos = env.env.sim.data.body(base_body_name).xpos
    except:
        # Fallback: use default robot position
        base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    # Get robot base orientation in world frame
    try:
        base_quat_wxyz = env.env.sim.data.body(base_body_name).xquat
        base_rot = R.from_quat(base_quat_wxyz[[1, 2, 3, 0]])  # Convert to xyzw
    except:
        base_rot = R.from_quat([0, 0, 0, 1])
    
    # Transform position from robot base frame to world frame
    pos_world = base_pos + base_rot.apply(pos_robot_base)
    
    # Transform orientation from robot base frame to world frame
    cam_rot_robot = R.from_quat(quat_xyzw_robot_base)
    cam_rot_world = base_rot * cam_rot_robot
    quat_wxyz_world = cam_rot_world.as_quat()[[3, 0, 1, 2]]  # Convert to wxyz
    
    # Set the camera pose
    camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=pos_world, quat=quat_wxyz_world)


def main():
    """
    Main function to demonstrate camera movement in Robocasa.
    """
    print("=" * 60)
    print("Robocasa Camera Movement Demo")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    # Configuration
    VIDEO_WIDTH = 640
    VIDEO_HEIGHT = 480
    NUM_FRAMES = 300
    FPS = 30
    USE_SPIRAL = True  # Set to False for circular trajectory
    
    # Create environment with offscreen rendering
    print("\n[1/5] Creating Robocasa environment...")
    
    env = robosuite.make(
        "PickPlaceCounterToCabinet",
        robots="Panda",
        controller="OSC_POSE",
        gripper_types="default",
        initialization_noise="default",
        use_camera_obs=False,
        has_renderer=False,
        has_offscreen_renderer=True,
        render_camera="frontview",  # Initial camera
        render_collision_mesh=False,
        render_visual_mesh=True,
        control_freq=20,
        horizon=NUM_FRAMES + 10,
        ignore_done=True,
    )
    
    print(f"   Environment created: {env.name}")
    
    # Create camera mover for dynamic camera control
    print("\n[2/5] Setting up camera mover...")
    
    camera_mover = CameraMover(
        env=env,
        camera="frontview",
        init_camera_pos=[1.0, 0.0, 1.2],
        init_camera_quat=[0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5],  # xyzw format
    )
    
    print("   Camera mover initialized")
    
    # Generate camera trajectory
    print("\n[3/5] Generating camera trajectory...")
    
    if USE_SPIRAL:
        trajectory = create_spiral_trajectory(
            num_frames=NUM_FRAMES,
            start_radius=2.0,
            end_radius=0.6,
            start_height=1.5,
            end_height=0.7,
            num_rotations=2
        )
        print("   Using spiral trajectory (inward)")
    else:
        trajectory = create_camera_trajectory(
            num_frames=NUM_FRAMES,
            radius=1.5,
            height_range=(0.8, 1.5)
        )
        print("   Using circular trajectory")
    
    # Reset environment
    env.reset()
    
    # Create video writer
    print("\n[4/5] Rendering camera trajectory...")
    
    output_video = "camera_trajectory.mp4"
    fourcc = cv2.VideoWriter_fourcc(*'mp4v')
    out = cv2.VideoWriter(output_video, fourcc, FPS, (VIDEO_WIDTH, VIDEO_HEIGHT))
    
    # Render each frame with updated camera pose
    for frame_idx, (pos, quat) in enumerate(trajectory):
        # Set camera pose (in robot base frame)
        # For this demo, we're using world frame coordinates directly
        # In a real application, use set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame()
        camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=pos, quat=quat[[3, 0, 1, 2]])  # Convert to wxyz
        
        # Step the environment (minimal action to keep robot stable)
        action = np.zeros(env.action_spec[0].shape)
        obs, reward, done, info = env.step(action)
        
        # Render the current frame
        frame = env.sim.render(
            height=VIDEO_HEIGHT,
            width=VIDEO_WIDTH,
            camera_name="frontview",
        )[::-1]  # Flip vertically
        
        # Convert to BGR for OpenCV
        frame_bgr = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_RGB2BGR)
        
        # Add frame info
        cv2.putText(
            frame_bgr,
            f"Frame: {frame_idx + 1}/{NUM_FRAMES}",
            (10, 30),
            cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX,
            0.7,
            (0, 255, 0),
            2
        )
        
        # Add camera position info
        cv2.putText(
            frame_bgr,
            f"Cam: ({pos[0]:.2f}, {pos[1]:.2f}, {pos[2]:.2f})",
            (10, 60),
            cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX,
            0.5,
            (0, 255, 0),
            1
        )
        
        # Write frame to video
        out.write(frame_bgr)
        
        # Progress indicator
        if (frame_idx + 1) % 50 == 0:
            print(f"   Rendered {frame_idx + 1}/{NUM_FRAMES} frames...")
    
    # Release video writer
    out.release()
    
    # Cleanup
    env.close()
    
    print("\n[5/5] Complete!")
    print(f"   Video saved to: {os.path.abspath(output_video)}")
    print(f"   Video info: {NUM_FRAMES} frames @ {FPS} FPS ({NUM_FRAMES/FPS:.1f}s)")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    return output_video


def example_robot_base_frame_usage():
    """
    Example showing how to set camera poses in robot base frame.
    
    This is the recommended approach for collecting training data that needs
    to be aligned with robot manipulation data.
    """
    # Create environment
    env = robosuite.make(
        "PickPlaceCounterToCabinet",
        robots="Panda",
        has_renderer=False,
        has_offscreen_renderer=True,
        use_camera_obs=False,
    )
    
    # Create camera mover
    camera_mover = CameraMover(env=env, camera="frontview")
    
    env.reset()
    
    # Example 1: Camera positioned relative to robot base
    # Position: 0.5m in front, 0.3m to the left, 1.0m above robot base
    # Orientation: Looking down at 45 degrees
    pos_robot_base = np.array([0.5, -0.3, 1.0])
    
    # Create quaternion for looking down at 45 degrees
    rot = R.from_euler('xyz', [30, 0, 0], degrees=True)  # Tilt down 30 degrees
    quat_robot_base = rot.as_quat()  # xyzw format
    
    # Set camera pose (automatically transforms to world frame)
    set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(
        env, camera_mover, pos_robot_base, quat_robot_base
    )
    
    # Render
    frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]
    
    # Example 2: Multiple camera poses for data collection
    camera_poses_robot_base = [
        # (position_xyz, quaternion_xyzw)
        (np.array([0.5, 0.0, 1.2]), R.from_euler('xyz', [20, 0, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.6, 0.3, 1.0]), R.from_euler('xyz', [25, -10, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.6, -0.3, 1.0]), R.from_euler('xyz', [25, 10, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.4, 0.0, 0.8]), R.from_euler('xyz', [45, 0, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
    ]
    
    for pos, quat in camera_poses_robot_base:
        set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(env, camera_mover, pos, quat)
        frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]
        # Process frame...
    
    env.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
    
    # Uncomment to see robot base frame example
    # example_robot_base_frame_usage()"}

{"path": "/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/CAMERA_MOVEMENT_GUIDE.md", "content": "# Robocasa Camera Movement Implementation Guide

This document describes how to implement dynamic camera movement in Robocasa for collecting training data for next best view selection models in robot manipulation.

## Overview

Robocasa (built on Robosuite/MuJoCo) allows dynamic camera pose control through the `CameraMover` utility class. This enables setting different camera poses at each timestep, which is essential for:

- Collecting multi-view training data
- Training next best view selection models
- Active perception research
- Visual servoing applications

## Key Interfaces

### 1. CameraMover Class

The `CameraMover` class (from `robosuite.utils.camera_utils`) is the primary interface for dynamic camera control.

#### Initialization

```python
from robosuite.utils.camera_utils import CameraMover

camera_mover = CameraMover(
    env=env,                    # Robosuite/Robocasa environment
    camera="frontview",         # Name of camera to control
    init_camera_pos=[x, y, z],  # Initial position (optional)
    init_camera_quat=[x, y, z, w]  # Initial orientation in xyzw format (optional)
)
```

**Important:** The `CameraMover` will re-initialize the environment by modifying the XML to attach the camera to a movable mocap body.

#### Setting Camera Pose

```python
# Set position only
camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=[x, y, z])

# Set orientation only (wxyz format for MuJoCo)
camera_mover.set_camera_pose(quat=[w, x, y, z])

# Set both position and orientation
camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=[x, y, z], quat=[w, x, y, z])
```

**Note:** MuJoCo uses wxyz quaternion format, while scipy/standard libraries use xyzw. Use appropriate conversions.

#### Getting Camera Pose

```python
pos, quat_wxyz = camera_mover.get_camera_pose()
```

### 2. Environment Setup

For offscreen rendering (required for data collection):

```python
import robosuite

env = robosuite.make(
    "PickPlaceCounterToCabinet",  # Task name
    robots="Panda",
    has_renderer=False,            # No on-screen viewer
    has_offscreen_renderer=True,   # Enable offscreen rendering
    use_camera_obs=False,          # Don't use camera observations (manual control)
    render_camera="frontview",     # Default camera for rendering
    render_collision_mesh=False,   # Optional: hide collision meshes
    render_visual_mesh=True,       # Show visual meshes
    ignore_done=True,              # Continue past task completion
    horizon=1000,                  # Episode length
)
```

### 3. Rendering Frames

```python
# Render from specific camera
frame = env.sim.render(
    height=480,
    width=640,
    camera_name="frontview",
)[::-1]  # Flip vertically (MuJoCo convention)

# Frame is RGB numpy array, shape: (height, width, 3)
```

## Coordinate Systems

### Robot Base Frame vs World Frame

For robot manipulation data, it's recommended to express camera poses in the **robot base frame** rather than the world frame. This ensures consistency across different robot placements.

#### Transformation from Robot Base to World Frame

```python
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R
import numpy as np

def set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(env, camera_mover, pos_robot_base, quat_xyzw_robot_base):
    """
    Set camera pose using coordinates in the robot base frame.
    
    Args:
        env: Robocasa environment
        camera_mover: CameraMover instance
        pos_robot_base: Camera position in robot base frame (x, y, z)
        quat_xyzw_robot_base: Camera orientation in robot base frame (x, y, z, w)
    """
    # Get robot base in world frame
    robot = env.robots[0]
    base_body_name = robot.robot_model.naming_prefix + "mobilebase"
    
    base_pos = env.sim.data.body(base_body_name).xpos
    base_quat_wxyz = env.sim.data.body(base_body_name).xquat
    base_rot = R.from_quat(base_quat_wxyz[[1, 2, 3, 0]])  # Convert to xyzw
    
    # Transform position
    pos_world = base_pos + base_rot.apply(pos_robot_base)
    
    # Transform orientation
    cam_rot_robot = R.from_quat(quat_xyzw_robot_base)
    cam_rot_world = base_rot * cam_rot_robot
    quat_wxyz_world = cam_rot_world.as_quat()[[3, 0, 1, 2]]  # Convert to wxyz
    
    # Set camera pose
    camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=pos_world, quat=quat_wxyz_world)
```

### Quaternion Format

| Library/Format | Order | Notes |
|---------------|-------|-------|
| MuJoCo/Robosuite | `[w, x, y, z]` | Used in `set_camera_pose()` |
| scipy Rotation | `[x, y, z, w]` | Used in `R.as_quat()` |
| Standard (Hamilton) | `[x, y, z, w]` | Most common |

**Conversion:**
```python
# xyzw to wxyz
quat_wxyz = quat_xyzw[[3, 0, 1, 2]]

# wxyz to xyzw
quat_xyzw = quat_wxyz[[1, 2, 3, 0]]
```

## Usage Examples

### Example 1: Basic Camera Movement

```python
import robosuite
from robosuite.utils.camera_utils import CameraMover
import numpy as np

# Create environment
env = robosuite.make(
    "PickPlaceCounterToCabinet",
    robots="Panda",
    has_renderer=False,
    has_offscreen_renderer=True,
    use_camera_obs=False,
)

# Create camera mover
camera_mover = CameraMover(env=env, camera="frontview")

# Reset environment
env.reset()

# Move camera in a circle
for i in range(100):
    angle = 2 * np.pi * i / 100
    
    # Circular path
    pos = [1.0 * np.cos(angle), 1.0 * np.sin(angle), 1.2]
    
    # Look at origin
    direction = -np.array(pos)
    direction /= np.linalg.norm(direction)
    
    # Simple orientation (adjust as needed)
    quat = [0.707, 0, 0, 0.707]  # wxyz
    
    camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=pos, quat=quat)
    
    # Step environment
    action = np.zeros(env.action_spec[0].shape)
    env.step(action)
    
    # Render
    frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]

env.close()
```

### Example 2: Multiple Fixed Views

```python
# Define camera poses in robot base frame
camera_poses = [
    {"name": "front", "pos": [0.5, 0.0, 1.2], "euler": [20, 0, 0]},
    {"name": "left", "pos": [0.5, 0.4, 1.0], "euler": [25, -15, 0]},
    {"name": "right", "pos": [0.5, -0.4, 1.0], "euler": [25, 15, 0]},

for view in camera_poses:
    # Convert euler angles to quaternion
    rot = R.from_euler('xyz', view["euler"], degrees=True)
    quat_xyzw = rot.as_quat()
    
    # Set camera pose
    set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(
        env, camera_mover, 
        np.array(view["pos"]), 
        quat_xyzw
    )
    
    # Capture frame
    frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]
    
    # Save or process frame...
```

### Example 3: Integration with Data Collection Pipeline

```python
def collect_episode_with_camera_trajectory(env_name, num_frames=300):
    """Collect one episode with dynamic camera movement."""
    
    # Create environment
    env = robosuite.make(
        env_name,
        robots="Panda",
        has_renderer=False,
        has_offscreen_renderer=True,
        use_camera_obs=False,
        ignore_done=True,
        horizon=num_frames,
    )
    
    camera_mover = CameraMover(env=env, camera="frontview")
    env.reset()
    
    episode_data = {
        "frames": [],
        "camera_poses": [],
        "actions": [],
        "observations": [],
    }
    
    for t in range(num_frames):
        # Calculate camera pose for this timestep
        t_norm = t / num_frames
        pos = calculate_camera_pose(t_norm)  # Your trajectory function
        quat = calculate_camera_orientation(t_norm, pos)
        
        # Set camera pose
        camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=pos, quat=quat)
        
        # Execute action (from policy or random)
        action = policy.get_action(observation)  # or np.random.randn(...)
        obs, reward, done, info = env.step(action)
        
        # Render frame
        frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]
        
        # Store data
        episode_data["frames"].append(frame)
        episode_data["camera_poses"].append({"pos": pos.tolist(), "quat": quat.tolist()})
        episode_data["actions"].append(action)
        episode_data["observations"].append(obs)
    
    env.close()
    return episode_data
```

## Available Cameras in Robocasa

Robocasa environments come with several pre-configured cameras:

| Camera Name | Description | Typical Use |
|------------|-------------|-------------|
| `agentview` | Third-person view of robot | General observation |
| `agentview_center` | Centered third-person view | Main training view |
| `agentview_left` | Left offset third-person | Multi-view training |
| `agentview_right` | Right offset third-person | Multi-view training |
| `frontview` | Front view of workspace | Manipulation tasks |
| `eye_in_hand` | Wrist-mounted camera | Visual servoing |

You can also add custom cameras using `env.mujoco_arena.set_camera()`.

## Performance Considerations

1. **Offscreen Rendering**: Always use `has_offscreen_renderer=True` for data collection. On-screen rendering is much slower.

2. **CameraMover Overhead**: The `CameraMover` modifies the environment XML once during initialization. Subsequent pose changes are fast.

3. **Batch Rendering**: For large-scale data collection, consider rendering multiple frames before saving to disk.

4. **Resolution**: Use the minimum resolution needed for your application. Higher resolutions significantly increase rendering time.

## Troubleshooting

### Camera Not Moving

- Ensure `CameraMover` is initialized after environment creation
- Call `env.sim.forward()` after setting pose if changes don't appear
- Verify camera name matches an existing camera in the environment

### Quaternion Orientation Issues

- Check quaternion format (wxyz vs xyzw)
- Verify coordinate system (robot base vs world)
- Use `camera_mover.get_camera_pose()` to debug current pose

### Rendering Errors

- Ensure `has_offscreen_renderer=True`
- Check that camera name exists: `env.sim.model.camera_names`
- For EGL rendering issues, set `render_gpu_device_id` if multiple GPUs

## Complete Example Script

See `robocasa_camera_movement.py` for a complete working example that:
- Creates a Robocasa kitchen environment
- Sets up dynamic camera control
- Generates smooth camera trajectories (circular and spiral)
- Renders and saves output as MP4 video

## References

- Robosuite Documentation: https://robosuite.ai/
- Robocasa GitHub: https://github.com/robocasa/robocasa
- MuJoCo Camera Documentation: http://www.mujoco.org/book/modeling.html#camera"}

def set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(env, camera_mover, pos_robot_base, quat_xyzw_robot_base):
    """
    Set camera pose using coordinates in the robot base frame.
    
    This function transforms the camera pose from the robot base frame to the
    world frame before setting it.
    
    Args:
        env: Robocasa environment
        camera_mover: CameraMover instance
        pos_robot_base: Camera position in robot base frame (x, y, z)
        quat_xyzw_robot_base: Camera orientation in robot base frame (x, y, z, w)
    """
    # Get robot base position in world frame
    # In Robocasa, the robot base is typically at a known offset from world origin
    # For most kitchen environments, we need to get the mobile base position
    
    # Get the robot's base position
    robot = env.env.robots[0]
    
    # Get robot base position in world frame
    # The mobile base position can be obtained from the robot model
    try:
        # Try to get mobile base body
        base_body_name = robot.robot_model.naming_prefix + "mobilebase"
        base_pos = env.env.sim.data.body(base_body_name).xpos
    except:
        # Fallback: use default robot position
        base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    # Get robot base orientation in world frame
    try:
        base_quat_wxyz = env.env.sim.data.body(base_body_name).xquat
        base_rot = R.from_quat(base_quat_wxyz[[1, 2, 3, 0]])  # Convert to xyzw
    except:
        base_rot = R.from_quat([0, 0, 0, 1])
    
    # Transform position from robot base frame to world frame
    pos_world = base_pos + base_rot.apply(pos_robot_base)
    
    # Transform orientation from robot base frame to world frame
    cam_rot_robot = R.from_quat(quat_xyzw_robot_base)
    cam_rot_world = base_rot * cam_rot_robot
    quat_wxyz_world = cam_rot_world.as_quat()[[3, 0, 1, 2]]  # Convert to wxyz
    
    # Set the camera pose
    camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=pos_world, quat=quat_wxyz_world)

{"path": "/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/robocasa_camera_movement.py", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Robocasa/Robosuite Camera Movement Example Script

This script demonstrates how to dynamically set camera poses at each timestep
in Robosuite/Robocasa for collecting training data for next best view selection models.

The camera poses are expressed in absolute coordinates in the robot base frame.

Usage:
    python robocasa_camera_movement.py

Output:
    - camera_trajectory.mp4: Video showing smooth camera trajectory movement
"""

import os
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R
import cv2

# Suppress warnings
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning)
os.environ['MUJOCO_GL'] = 'egl'  # Use EGL for offscreen rendering

import robosuite
from robosuite.utils.camera_utils import CameraMover


def create_camera_trajectory(num_frames=300, radius=1.5, height_range=(0.8, 1.5)):
    """
    Create a smooth camera trajectory around the robot workspace.
    
    Args:
        num_frames: Number of frames in the trajectory
        radius: Radius of the circular trajectory around the robot
        height_range: (min_height, max_height) for vertical movement
        
    Returns:
        List of (position, quaternion) tuples for each frame
    """
    trajectory = []
    
    # Robot base position (approximately where the robot is located)
    robot_base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    for i in range(num_frames):
        # Calculate progress through trajectory (0 to 1)
        t = i / num_frames
        
        # Circular motion around the robot
        angle = 2 * np.pi * t
        
        # Position: circular path with varying height
        x = robot_base_pos[0] + radius * np.cos(angle)
        y = robot_base_pos[1] + radius * np.sin(angle)
        z = height_range[0] + (height_range[1] - height_range[0]) * 0.5 * (1 + np.sin(2 * np.pi * t))
        
        pos = np.array([x, y, z])
        
        # Orientation: always look at the robot base
        # Calculate direction vector from camera to target
        direction = robot_base_pos - pos
        direction[2] += 0.3  # Look slightly above the base
        
        # Create rotation matrix that looks at the target
        # Up vector is approximately +z
        up = np.array([0, 0, 1])
        
        # Calculate right, up, forward vectors
        forward = direction / np.linalg.norm(direction)
        right = np.cross(forward, up)
        right = right / (np.linalg.norm(right) + 1e-8)
        up = np.cross(right, forward)
        
        # Create rotation matrix
        rot_matrix = np.column_stack([right, up, forward])
        
        # Convert to quaternion (xyzw format for robosuite)
        quat_xyzw = R.from_matrix(rot_matrix).as_quat()
        
        trajectory.append((pos, quat_xyzw))
    
    return trajectory


def create_spiral_trajectory(num_frames=300, start_radius=2.0, end_radius=0.8, 
                              start_height=1.5, end_height=0.6, num_rotations=2):
    """
    Create a spiral camera trajectory that moves inward toward the robot.
    
    Args:
        num_frames: Number of frames in the trajectory
        start_radius: Starting radius of the spiral
        end_radius: Ending radius of the spiral
        start_height: Starting height
        end_height: Ending height
        num_rotations: Number of complete rotations in the spiral
        
    Returns:
        List of (position, quaternion) tuples for each frame
    """
    trajectory = []
    
    robot_base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    for i in range(num_frames):
        t = i / num_frames
        
        # Spiral parameters
        angle = 2 * np.pi * num_rotations * t
        radius = start_radius + (end_radius - start_radius) * t
        height = start_height + (end_height - start_height) * t
        
        # Position
        x = robot_base_pos[0] + radius * np.cos(angle)
        y = robot_base_pos[1] + radius * np.sin(angle)
        z = height
        
        pos = np.array([x, y, z])
        
        # Orientation: look at robot base
        direction = robot_base_pos - pos
        direction[2] += 0.2
        
        up = np.array([0, 0, 1])
        forward = direction / np.linalg.norm(direction)
        right = np.cross(forward, up)
        right = right / (np.linalg.norm(right) + 1e-8)
        up = np.cross(right, forward)
        
        rot_matrix = np.column_stack([right, up, forward])
        quat_xyzw = R.from_matrix(rot_matrix).as_quat()
        
        trajectory.append((pos, quat_xyzw))
    
    return trajectory


def set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(env, camera_mover, pos_robot_base, quat_xyzw_robot_base):
    """
    Set camera pose using coordinates in the robot base frame.
    
    This function transforms the camera pose from the robot base frame to the
    world frame before setting it.
    
    Args:
        env: Robosuite/Robocasa environment
        camera_mover: CameraMover instance
        pos_robot_base: Camera position in robot base frame (x, y, z)
        quat_xyzw_robot_base: Camera orientation in robot base frame (x, y, z, w)
    """
    # Get robot base position in world frame
    robot = env.robots[0]
    
    # Get robot base position in world frame
    try:
        # Try to get mobile base body
        base_body_name = robot.robot_model.naming_prefix + "mobilebase"
        base_pos = env.sim.data.body(base_body_name).xpos
    except:
        # Fallback: use default robot position
        base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    # Get robot base orientation in world frame
    try:
        base_quat_wxyz = env.sim.data.body(base_body_name).xquat
        base_rot = R.from_quat(base_quat_wxyz[[1, 2, 3, 0]])  # Convert to xyzw
    except:
        base_rot = R.from_quat([0, 0, 0, 1])
    
    # Transform position from robot base frame to world frame
    pos_world = base_pos + base_rot.apply(pos_robot_base)
    
    # Transform orientation from robot base frame to world frame
    cam_rot_robot = R.from_quat(quat_xyzw_robot_base)
    cam_rot_world = base_rot * cam_rot_robot
    quat_wxyz_world = cam_rot_world.as_quat()[[3, 0, 1, 2]]  # Convert to wxyz
    
    # Set the camera pose
    camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=pos_world, quat=quat_wxyz_world)


def main():
    """
    Main function to demonstrate camera movement in Robosuite/Robocasa.
    """
    print("=" * 60)
    print("Robosuite/Robocasa Camera Movement Demo")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    # Configuration
    VIDEO_WIDTH = 640
    VIDEO_HEIGHT = 480
    NUM_FRAMES = 300
    FPS = 30
    USE_SPIRAL = True  # Set to False for circular trajectory
    
    # Create environment with offscreen rendering
    print("\n[1/5] Creating Robosuite environment (Kitchen scene)...")
    
    # Use a manipulation environment that's available in robosuite
    env = robosuite.make(
        "Lift",  # Simple manipulation task
        robots="Panda",
        gripper_types="PandaGripper",
        initialization_noise=0.0,
        use_camera_obs=False,
        has_renderer=False,
        has_offscreen_renderer=True,
        render_camera="frontview",
        render_collision_mesh=False,
        render_visual_mesh=True,
        control_freq=20,
        horizon=NUM_FRAMES + 10,
        ignore_done=True,
    )
    
    print(f"   Environment created: {env.name}")
    
    # Create camera mover for dynamic camera control
    print("\n[2/5] Setting up camera mover...")
    
    camera_mover = CameraMover(
        env=env,
        camera="frontview",
        init_camera_pos=[1.0, 0.0, 1.2],
        init_camera_quat=[0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5],  # xyzw format
    )
    
    print("   Camera mover initialized")
    
    # Generate camera trajectory
    print("\n[3/5] Generating camera trajectory...")
    
    if USE_SPIRAL:
        trajectory = create_spiral_trajectory(
            num_frames=NUM_FRAMES,
            start_radius=2.0,
            end_radius=0.6,
            start_height=1.5,
            end_height=0.7,
            num_rotations=2
        )
        print("   Using spiral trajectory (inward)")
    else:
        trajectory = create_camera_trajectory(
            num_frames=NUM_FRAMES,
            radius=1.5,
            height_range=(0.8, 1.5)
        )
        print("   Using circular trajectory")
    
    # Reset environment
    env.reset()
    
    # Create video writer
    print("\n[4/5] Rendering camera trajectory...")
    
    output_video = "camera_trajectory.mp4"
    fourcc = cv2.VideoWriter_fourcc(*'mp4v')
    out = cv2.VideoWriter(output_video, fourcc, FPS, (VIDEO_WIDTH, VIDEO_HEIGHT))
    
    # Render each frame with updated camera pose
    for frame_idx, (pos, quat) in enumerate(trajectory):
        # Set camera pose (in world frame for this demo)
        # For robot base frame, use set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame()
        camera_mover.set_camera_pose(pos=pos, quat=quat[[3, 0, 1, 2]])  # Convert to wxyz
        
        # Step the environment (minimal action to keep robot stable)
        action = np.zeros(env.action_spec[0].shape)
        obs, reward, done, info = env.step(action)
        
        # Render the current frame
        frame = env.sim.render(
            height=VIDEO_HEIGHT,
            width=VIDEO_WIDTH,
            camera_name="frontview",
        )[::-1]  # Flip vertically
        
        # Convert to BGR for OpenCV
        frame_bgr = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_RGB2BGR)
        
        # Add frame info
        cv2.putText(
            frame_bgr,
            f"Frame: {frame_idx + 1}/{NUM_FRAMES}",
            (10, 30),
            cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX,
            0.7,
            (0, 255, 0),
            2
        )
        
        # Add camera position info
        cv2.putText(
            frame_bgr,
            f"Cam: ({pos[0]:.2f}, {pos[1]:.2f}, {pos[2]:.2f})",
            (10, 60),
            cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX,
            0.5,
            (0, 255, 0),
            1
        )
        
        # Write frame to video
        out.write(frame_bgr)
        
        # Progress indicator
        if (frame_idx + 1) % 50 == 0:
            print(f"   Rendered {frame_idx + 1}/{NUM_FRAMES} frames...")
    
    # Release video writer
    out.release()
    
    # Cleanup
    env.close()
    
    print("\n[5/5] Complete!")
    print(f"   Video saved to: {os.path.abspath(output_video)}")
    print(f"   Video info: {NUM_FRAMES} frames @ {FPS} FPS ({NUM_FRAMES/FPS:.1f}s)")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    return output_video


def example_robot_base_frame_usage():
    """
    Example showing how to set camera poses in robot base frame.
    
    This is the recommended approach for collecting training data that needs
    to be aligned with robot manipulation data.
    """
    # Create environment
    env = robosuite.make(
        "Lift",
        robots="Panda",
        gripper_types="PandaGripper",
        has_renderer=False,
        has_offscreen_renderer=True,
        use_camera_obs=False,
    )
    
    # Create camera mover
    camera_mover = CameraMover(env=env, camera="frontview")
    
    env.reset()
    
    # Example 1: Camera positioned relative to robot base
    # Position: 0.5m in front, 0.3m to the left, 1.0m above robot base
    # Orientation: Looking down at 45 degrees
    pos_robot_base = np.array([0.5, -0.3, 1.0])
    
    # Create quaternion for looking down at 45 degrees
    rot = R.from_euler('xyz', [30, 0, 0], degrees=True)  # Tilt down 30 degrees
    quat_robot_base = rot.as_quat()  # xyzw format
    
    # Set camera pose (automatically transforms to world frame)
    set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(
        env, camera_mover, pos_robot_base, quat_robot_base
    )
    
    # Render
    frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]
    
    # Example 2: Multiple camera poses for data collection
    camera_poses_robot_base = [
        # (position_xyz, quaternion_xyzw)
        (np.array([0.5, 0.0, 1.2]), R.from_euler('xyz', [20, 0, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.6, 0.3, 1.0]), R.from_euler('xyz', [25, -10, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.6, -0.3, 1.0]), R.from_euler('xyz', [25, 10, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.4, 0.0, 0.8]), R.from_euler('xyz', [45, 0, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
    ]
    
    for pos, quat in camera_poses_robot_base:
        set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(env, camera_mover, pos, quat)
        frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]
        # Process frame...
    
    env.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
    
    # Uncomment to see robot base frame example
    # example_robot_base_frame_usage()"}

{"path": "/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/robocasa_camera_movement.py", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Robocasa/Robosuite Camera Movement Example Script

This script demonstrates how to dynamically set camera poses at each timestep
in Robosuite/Robocasa for collecting training data for next best view selection models.

The camera poses are expressed in absolute coordinates in the robot base frame.

Usage:
    python robocasa_camera_movement.py

Output:
    - camera_trajectory.mp4: Video showing smooth camera trajectory movement
"""

import os
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R
import cv2

# Suppress warnings
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning)
os.environ['MUJOCO_GL'] = 'egl'  # Use EGL for offscreen rendering

import robosuite


def create_camera_trajectory(num_frames=300, radius=1.5, height_range=(0.8, 1.5)):
    """
    Create a smooth camera trajectory around the robot workspace.
    
    Args:
        num_frames: Number of frames in the trajectory
        radius: Radius of the circular trajectory around the robot
        height_range: (min_height, max_height) for vertical movement
        
    Returns:
        List of (position, quaternion) tuples for each frame
    """
    trajectory = []
    
    # Robot base position (approximately where the robot is located)
    robot_base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    for i in range(num_frames):
        # Calculate progress through trajectory (0 to 1)
        t = i / num_frames
        
        # Circular motion around the robot
        angle = 2 * np.pi * t
        
        # Position: circular path with varying height
        x = robot_base_pos[0] + radius * np.cos(angle)
        y = robot_base_pos[1] + radius * np.sin(angle)
        z = height_range[0] + (height_range[1] - height_range[0]) * 0.5 * (1 + np.sin(2 * np.pi * t))
        
        pos = np.array([x, y, z])
        
        # Orientation: look at robot base using look_at style rotation
        target = robot_base_pos + np.array([0, 0, 0.3])
        
        # Forward vector (from camera to target)
        forward = target - pos
        forward = forward / np.linalg.norm(forward)
        
        # World up vector
        world_up = np.array([0, 0, 1])
        
        # Right vector
        right = np.cross(forward, world_up)
        if np.linalg.norm(right) < 1e-6:
            world_up = np.array([0, 1, 0])
        right = np.cross(forward, world_up)
        right = right / np.linalg.norm(right)
        
        # True up vector
        up = np.cross(right, forward)
        
        # Build rotation matrix (camera looks along -Z)
        rot_matrix = np.column_stack([right, up, -forward])
        
        # Ensure proper rotation matrix
        if np.linalg.det(rot_matrix) < 0:
            rot_matrix[:, 2] = -rot_matrix[:, 2]
        
        quat_xyzw = R.from_matrix(rot_matrix).as_quat()
        
        trajectory.append((pos, quat_xyzw))
    
    return trajectory


def create_spiral_trajectory(num_frames=300, start_radius=2.0, end_radius=0.8, 
                              start_height=1.5, end_height=0.6, num_rotations=2):
    """
    Create a spiral camera trajectory that moves inward toward the robot.
    
    Args:
        num_frames: Number of frames in the trajectory
        start_radius: Starting radius of the spiral
        end_radius: Ending radius of the spiral
        start_height: Starting height
        end_height: Ending height
        num_rotations: Number of complete rotations in the spiral
        
    Returns:
        List of (position, quaternion) tuples for each frame
    """
    trajectory = []
    
    robot_base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    for i in range(num_frames):
        t = i / num_frames
        
        # Spiral parameters
        angle = 2 * np.pi * num_rotations * t
        radius = start_radius + (end_radius - start_radius) * t
        height = start_height + (end_height - start_height) * t
        
        # Position
        x = robot_base_pos[0] + radius * np.cos(angle)
        y = robot_base_pos[1] + radius * np.sin(angle)
        z = height
        
        pos = np.array([x, y, z])
        
        # Orientation: look at robot base using look_at style rotation
        # Target point slightly above origin
        target = robot_base_pos + np.array([0, 0, 0.3])
        
        # Forward vector (from camera to target)
        forward = target - pos
        forward = forward / np.linalg.norm(forward)
        
        # World up vector
        world_up = np.array([0, 0, 1])
        
        # Right vector (perpendicular to forward and up)
        right = np.cross(forward, world_up)
        if np.linalg.norm(right) < 1e-6:
            # If forward is parallel to world_up, use different up
            world_up = np.array([0, 1, 0])
        right = np.cross(forward, world_up)
        right = right / np.linalg.norm(right)
        
        # True up vector (perpendicular to forward and right)
        up = np.cross(right, forward)
        
        # Build rotation matrix (column-major: right, up, -forward for camera frame)
        # Camera looks along -Z in OpenGL convention
        rot_matrix = np.column_stack([right, up, -forward])
        
        # Ensure proper rotation matrix (determinant = 1)
        if np.linalg.det(rot_matrix) < 0:
            rot_matrix[:, 2] = -rot_matrix[:, 2]
        
        quat_xyzw = R.from_matrix(rot_matrix).as_quat()
        
        trajectory.append((pos, quat_xyzw))
    
    return trajectory


def set_free_camera_pose(sim, pos, quat_wxyz):
    """
    Set the pose of a free camera in the simulation.
    
    This directly modifies the camera's position and orientation in the MuJoCo sim.
    
    Args:
        sim: MuJoCo simulator instance
        pos: Camera position (x, y, z) in world frame
        quat_wxyz: Camera orientation as quaternion [w, x, y, z]
    """
    cam_id = sim.model.camera_name2id("frontview")
    
    # Set camera position
    sim.model.cam_pos[cam_id] = pos
    
    # Set camera orientation (MuJoCo uses rotation matrix internally)
    rot = R.from_quat(quat_wxyz[[1, 2, 3, 0]])  # Convert wxyz to xyzw for scipy
    rot_matrix = rot.as_matrix()
    
    # MuJoCo stores camera orientation as a 3x3 rotation matrix in row-major order
    sim.model.cam_mat[cam_id] = rot_matrix.T.flatten()
    
    # Mark camera as free (not attached to any body)
    sim.model.cam_mode[cam_id] = 0  # fixed camera


def set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(sim, pos_robot_base, quat_xyzw_robot_base, robot_base_pos=None, robot_base_quat_wxyz=None):
    """
    Set camera pose using coordinates in the robot base frame.
    
    This function transforms the camera pose from the robot base frame to the
    world frame before setting it.
    
    Args:
        sim: MuJoCo simulator instance
        pos_robot_base: Camera position in robot base frame (x, y, z)
        quat_xyzw_robot_base: Camera orientation in robot base frame (x, y, z, w)
        robot_base_pos: Robot base position in world frame (default: [0,0,0])
        robot_base_quat_wxyz: Robot base orientation in world frame (default: identity)
    """
    if robot_base_pos is None:
        robot_base_pos = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    if robot_base_quat_wxyz is None:
        robot_base_quat_wxyz = np.array([1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
    
    # Convert robot base quaternion to rotation
    base_rot = R.from_quat(robot_base_quat_wxyz[[1, 2, 3, 0]])  # wxyz to xyzw
    
    # Transform position from robot base frame to world frame
    pos_world = robot_base_pos + base_rot.apply(pos_robot_base)
    
    # Transform orientation from robot base frame to world frame
    cam_rot_robot = R.from_quat(quat_xyzw_robot_base)
    cam_rot_world = base_rot * cam_rot_robot
    quat_wxyz_world = cam_rot_world.as_quat()[[3, 0, 1, 2]]  # xyzw to wxyz
    
    # Set the camera pose
    set_free_camera_pose(sim, pos_world, quat_wxyz_world)


def main():
    """
    Main function to demonstrate camera movement in Robosuite/Robocasa.
    """
    print("=" * 60)
    print("Robosuite/Robocasa Camera Movement Demo")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    # Configuration
    VIDEO_WIDTH = 640
    VIDEO_HEIGHT = 480
    NUM_FRAMES = 300
    FPS = 30
    USE_SPIRAL = True  # Set to False for circular trajectory
    
    # Create environment with offscreen rendering
    print("\n[1/5] Creating Robosuite environment...")
    
    # Use a manipulation environment
    env = robosuite.make(
        "Lift",  # Simple manipulation task
        robots="Panda",
        gripper_types="PandaGripper",
        initialization_noise={"magnitude": 0.0, "type": "uniform"},
        use_camera_obs=False,
        has_renderer=False,
        has_offscreen_renderer=True,
        render_camera="frontview",
        render_collision_mesh=False,
        render_visual_mesh=True,
        control_freq=20,
        horizon=NUM_FRAMES + 10,
        ignore_done=True,
    )
    
    print("   Environment created: Lift")
    
    # Generate camera trajectory
    print("\n[2/5] Generating camera trajectory...")
    
    if USE_SPIRAL:
        trajectory = create_spiral_trajectory(
            num_frames=NUM_FRAMES,
            start_radius=2.0,
            end_radius=0.6,
            start_height=1.5,
            end_height=0.7,
            num_rotations=2
        )
        print("   Using spiral trajectory (inward)")
    else:
        trajectory = create_camera_trajectory(
            num_frames=NUM_FRAMES,
            radius=1.5,
            height_range=(0.8, 1.5)
        )
        print("   Using circular trajectory")
    
    # Reset environment
    env.reset()
    
    # Create video writer
    print("\n[3/5] Rendering camera trajectory...")
    
    output_video = "camera_trajectory.mp4"
    fourcc = cv2.VideoWriter_fourcc(*'mp4v')
    out = cv2.VideoWriter(output_video, fourcc, FPS, (VIDEO_WIDTH, VIDEO_HEIGHT))
    
    # Render each frame with updated camera pose
    for frame_idx, (pos, quat) in enumerate(trajectory):
        # Set camera pose using free camera
        # Note: This modifies the camera directly in the sim
        quat_wxyz = quat[[3, 0, 1, 2]]  # Convert xyzw to wxyz
        set_free_camera_pose(env.sim, pos, quat_wxyz)
        
        # Step the environment (minimal action to keep robot stable)
        action = np.zeros(env.action_spec[0].shape)
        obs, reward, done, info = env.step(action)
        
        # Render the current frame
        frame = env.sim.render(
            height=VIDEO_HEIGHT,
            width=VIDEO_WIDTH,
            camera_name="frontview",
        )[::-1]  # Flip vertically
        
        # Convert to BGR for OpenCV
        frame_bgr = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_RGB2BGR)
        
        # Add frame info
        cv2.putText(
            frame_bgr,
            f"Frame: {frame_idx + 1}/{NUM_FRAMES}",
            (10, 30),
            cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX,
            0.7,
            (0, 255, 0),
            2
        )
        
        # Add camera position info
        cv2.putText(
            frame_bgr,
            f"Cam: ({pos[0]:.2f}, {pos[1]:.2f}, {pos[2]:.2f})",
            (10, 60),
            cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX,
            0.5,
            (0, 255, 0),
            1
        )
        
        # Write frame to video
        out.write(frame_bgr)
        
        # Progress indicator
        if (frame_idx + 1) % 50 == 0:
            print(f"   Rendered {frame_idx + 1}/{NUM_FRAMES} frames...")
    
    # Release video writer
    out.release()
    
    # Cleanup
    env.close()
    
    print("\n[4/5] Complete!")
    print(f"   Video saved to: {os.path.abspath(output_video)}")
    print(f"   Video info: {NUM_FRAMES} frames @ {FPS} FPS ({NUM_FRAMES/FPS:.1f}s)")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    return output_video


def example_robot_base_frame_usage():
    """
    Example showing how to set camera poses in robot base frame.
    
    This is the recommended approach for collecting training data that needs
    to be aligned with robot manipulation data.
    """
    # Create environment
    env = robosuite.make(
        "Lift",
        robots="Panda",
        gripper_types="PandaGripper",
        initialization_noise={"magnitude": 0.0, "type": "uniform"},
        has_renderer=False,
        has_offscreen_renderer=True,
        use_camera_obs=False,
    )
    
    env.reset()
    
    # Example 1: Camera positioned relative to robot base
    # Position: 0.5m in front, 0.3m to the left, 1.0m above robot base
    pos_robot_base = np.array([0.5, -0.3, 1.0])
    
    # Create quaternion for looking down at 30 degrees
    rot = R.from_euler('xyz', [30, 0, 0], degrees=True)
    quat_robot_base = rot.as_quat()  # xyzw format
    
    # Get robot base pose from simulation
    robot_base_body = env.sim.model.body_name2id("robot0_base")
    robot_base_pos = env.sim.data.body(robot_base_body).xpos
    robot_base_quat_wxyz = env.sim.data.body(robot_base_body).xquat
    
    # Set camera pose (automatically transforms to world frame)
    set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(
        env.sim, pos_robot_base, quat_robot_base,
        robot_base_pos, robot_base_quat_wxyz
    )
    
    # Render
    frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]
    
    # Example 2: Multiple camera poses for data collection
    camera_poses_robot_base = [
        (np.array([0.5, 0.0, 1.2]), R.from_euler('xyz', [20, 0, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.6, 0.3, 1.0]), R.from_euler('xyz', [25, -10, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.6, -0.3, 1.0]), R.from_euler('xyz', [25, 10, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
        (np.array([0.4, 0.0, 0.8]), R.from_euler('xyz', [45, 0, 0], degrees=True).as_quat()),
    ]
    
    for pos, quat in camera_poses_robot_base:
        set_camera_pose_in_robot_base_frame(
            env.sim, pos, quat,
            robot_base_pos, robot_base_quat_wxyz
        )
        frame = env.sim.render(height=480, width=640, camera_name="frontview")[::-1]
